asset_type: TaraHome technical blog cover, landscape editorial photograph
prompt: A homeowner carefully replaces a small unbranded USB Thread radio adapter on a compact home-automation computer while preserving the existing smart-home setup. Show a warm lived-in residential utility nook on a natural oak shelf, with subtle plants and soft furnishings out of focus. Use a close view of one human hand plugging a simple dark-green-accented USB radio adapter with a short extension cable into a compact mini computer; the old disconnected adapter rests beside it and ordinary smart-home lights remain calmly on in the background. Use clean photorealistic editorial home-technology photography, believable cable placement, warm late-afternoon window light, natural material texture, and restrained forest-green accents. Keep the main hand and replacement adapter centered for both full article and 16:9 card crops. No visible brand logos, text, watermark, screen interface, QR codes, embedded headline, charts, graphs, routing lines, network paths, glowing trails, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, futuristic laboratory, impossible cables, extra fingers, duplicate hardware, or oversaturated green.
